message:
Add swarm orchestration types for weighted multi-project allocation (bd-33tq7)

Defines core data types in internal/swarm/types.go:
- ProjectBeadCount: project path/name with bead count and tier
- ProjectAllocation: per-project agent allocation (CC/Cod/Gmi)
- SwarmPlan: complete execution plan with sessions and allocations
- SessionSpec/PaneSpec: tmux session and pane specifications
- SwarmState: runtime state tracking for running swarms
- PaneState: individual pane status and activity tracking
- LimitHitEvent/RespawnEvent: event recording for limits and respawns

All types include proper JSON tags and comprehensive test coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-32ck","title":"Feature: Extend checkpoints with assignments/progress","description":"## Overview
Checkpointing already exists. Extend it to capture **assignment/progress context** so restores are more useful for multi‑agent work.

## Scope
- Include current assignments (bead → agent) in checkpoint metadata
- Include BV snapshot summary (ready/blocked counts) at checkpoint time
- Ensure robot outputs (`--robot-checkpoint-*` if present) include these fields
- Keep existing checkpoint storage format backward‑compatible (additive fields)

## Tests
- Unit tests for new checkpoint fields (serialize/deserialize)
- E2E: create checkpoint during active assignments; restore; verify summary emitted

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-CHECKPOINT] session=%s assignments=%d ready=%d blocked=%d", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checkpoints remain readable even without new fields
- New fields present when assignment tracking is enabled
- Restore flow surfaces assignment summary","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:12.436057119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:46:21.091234924Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ck","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:35:20.994759649Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-332h","title":"Task: Implement file edit detection patterns","description":"## Purpose
Extend robot/patterns.go with patterns to detect file edits from agent output.

## Detection Patterns per Agent Type
var fileWritePatterns = map[AgentType][]string{
    AgentClaude: {
        'using the edit tool',
        'using the write tool',
        'Edited file:',
        'Created file:',
        'Writing to file:',
    },
    AgentCodex: {
        'writing to file',
        'edit.*file',
        'created file',
    },
    AgentGemini: {
        'Writing:',
        'Editing:',
        'Created:',
    },
}

## Path Extraction
func ExtractEditedFiles(output string, agentType AgentType) []string

Extract file paths from patterns like:
- 'Edited file: internal/cli/send.go'
- 'Writing to file /path/to/file.go'
- Tool use JSON with file_path field

## Acceptance Criteria
- Patterns detect 90%+ of file edits
- File paths extracted accurately
- Both relative and absolute paths handled
- False positives minimized

## Technical Notes
Use regexp.MustCompile for pattern matching
Test against actual agent output samples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:28.921638062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773414528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773257222Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-332h","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-33tq7","title":"Foundation: Define ProjectAllocation and SwarmPlan types","description":"## Purpose

Define the core data types that represent project allocation decisions and the overall swarm execution plan.

## Implementation

Create `internal/swarm/types.go`:

```go
package swarm

import "time"

// ProjectBeadCount represents a project and its open bead count
type ProjectBeadCount struct {
    Path      string `json:"path"`       // Absolute path to project
    Name      string `json:"name"`       // Project directory name
    OpenBeads int    `json:"open_beads"` // Count of open beads
    Tier      int    `json:"tier"`       // Calculated tier (1, 2, or 3)
}

// ProjectAllocation represents the agent allocation for a single project
type ProjectAllocation struct {
    Project     ProjectBeadCount `json:"project"`
    CCAgents    int              `json:"cc_agents"`
    CodAgents   int              `json:"cod_agents"`
    GmiAgents   int              `json:"gmi_agents"`
    TotalAgents int              `json:"total_agents"`
}

// SwarmPlan is the complete execution plan for the weighted swarm
type SwarmPlan struct {
    // Metadata
    CreatedAt   time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    ScanDir     string    `json:"scan_dir"`
    
    // Per-project allocations
    Allocations []ProjectAllocation `json:"allocations"`
    
    // Aggregate totals
    TotalCC     int `json:"total_cc"`
    TotalCod    int `json:"total_cod"`
    TotalGmi    int `json:"total_gmi"`
    TotalAgents int `json:"total_agents"`
    
    // Session structure
    SessionsPerType int `json:"sessions_per_type"`
    PanesPerSession int `json:"panes_per_session"` // Calculated: ceil(total/sessions)
    
    // Session names to create
    Sessions []SessionSpec `json:"sessions"`
}

// SessionSpec describes a tmux session to create
type SessionSpec struct {
    Name       string     `json:"name"`        // e.g., "cc_agents_1"
    AgentType  string     `json:"agent_type"`  // "cc", "cod", or "gmi"
    PaneCount  int        `json:"pane_count"`
    Panes      []PaneSpec `json:"panes"`
}

// PaneSpec describes a pane within a session
type PaneSpec struct {
    Index       int    `json:"index"`        // 1-based pane index
    Project     string `json:"project"`      // Which project this pane works on
    AgentType   string `json:"agent_type"`
    AgentIndex  int    `json:"agent_index"`  // Agent number within project
    LaunchCmd   string `json:"launch_cmd"`   // "cc", "cod", or "gmi"
}

// SwarmState tracks the runtime state of a running swarm
type SwarmState struct {
    Plan        *SwarmPlan           `json:"plan"`
    StartedAt   time.Time            `json:"started_at"`
    PaneStates  map[string]PaneState `json:"pane_states"` // key: "session:pane"
    LimitHits   []LimitHitEvent      `json:"limit_hits"`
    Respawns    []RespawnEvent       `json:"respawns"`
}

// PaneState tracks individual pane runtime state
type PaneState struct {
    SessionPane   string    `json:"session_pane"`  // "cc_agents_1:1.5"
    AgentType     string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project       string    `json:"project"`
    Status        string    `json:"status"`        // "running", "limit_hit", "respawning"
    LastActivity  time.Time `json:"last_activity"`
    LimitHitAt    *time.Time `json:"limit_hit_at,omitempty"`
    RespawnCount  int       `json:"respawn_count"`
}

// LimitHitEvent records when an agent hits usage limits
type LimitHitEvent struct {
    SessionPane string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType   string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project     string    `json:"project"`
    DetectedAt  time.Time `json:"detected_at"`
    Pattern     string    `json:"pattern"`        // Which pattern matched
}

// RespawnEvent records agent respawns
type RespawnEvent struct {
    SessionPane   string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType     string    `json:"agent_type"`
    RespawnedAt   time.Time `json:"respawned_at"`
    AccountRotated bool     `json:"account_rotated"`
    PreviousAccount string  `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
    NewAccount      string  `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
}
```

## Design Rationale

1. **Separation of Plan vs State**: SwarmPlan is immutable after creation; SwarmState tracks runtime
2. **JSON tags**: Enable robot mode output and state persistence
3. **1-based pane indices**: Match tmux conventions (panes start at 1, not 0)
4. **Hierarchical structure**: Plan → Sessions → Panes makes orchestration straightforward

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All types defined with proper JSON tags
- [ ] Types are well-documented with godoc comments
- [ ] Unit tests for any helper methods
- [ ] Types support JSON marshaling/unmarshaling","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:58:26.463622702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.162272188Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33tq7","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.162246510Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

```go
package swarm

import "time"

// ProjectBeadCount represents a project and its open bead count
type ProjectBeadCount struct {

}

}

// SessionSpec describes a tmux session to create

}

type PaneState struct {

    AgentType     string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project       string    `json:"project"`
    Status        string    `json:"status"`        // "running", "limit_hit", "respawning"
    LastActivity  time.Time `json:"last_activity"`
    LimitHitAt    *time.Time `json:"limit_hit_at,omitempty"`
    RespawnCount  int       `json:"respawn_count"`
}

// LimitHitEvent records when an agent hits usage limits
type LimitHitEvent struct {
    SessionPane string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType   string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project     string    `json:"project"`
    DetectedAt  time.Time `json:"detected_at"`
    Pattern     string    `json:"pattern"`        // Which pattern matched
}

// RespawnEvent records agent respawns
type RespawnEvent struct {
    SessionPane   string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType     string    `json:"agent_type"`
    RespawnedAt   time.Time `json:"respawned_at"`
    AccountRotated bool     `json:"account_rotated"`
    PreviousAccount string  `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
    NewAccount      string  `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
}
```

## Design Rationale

1. **Separation of Plan vs State**: SwarmPlan is immutable after creation; SwarmState tracks runtime
2. **JSON tags**: Enable robot mode output and state persistence
3. **1-based pane indices**: Match tmux conventions (panes start at 1, not 0)
4. **Hierarchical structure**: Plan → Sessions → Panes makes orchestration straightforward

 {"id":"bd-33tuj","title":"Task: Add output_lines_since_last activity indicator","description":"Add output_lines_since_last field to pane status.

## What

Add `output_lines_since_last` field showing lines of output since last status check.

## Why

Quick activity indicator without timestamp parsing. Useful for:
- Detecting stalled agents (0 lines for extended period)
- Detecting hyperactive agents (excessive output)
- Progress monitoring

## Implementation

Track output line count between status calls:

```go
type OutputTracker struct {
    mu         sync.RWMutex
    lineCount  map[int]int  // pane_index -> total lines seen
}

func (t *OutputTracker) GetDelta(paneIndex int) int {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    
    current := countPaneLines(paneIndex)
    previous := t.lineCount[paneIndex]
    delta := current - previous
    t.lineCount[paneIndex] = current
    return delta
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "output_lines_since_last": 42
  }]
}
```

## Edge Cases

- First call: return total lines (no previous)
- tmux buffer wraps: handle potential negative delta
- Agent cleared screen: reset tracking","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:36:58.734360851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:50:37.002470134Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:50:37.002368092Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33tuj","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:36:58.767682243Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Testing

- Test with rano but missing capabilities
- Test without rano","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:14:24.457277743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:24:46.109067202Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:24:46.109007199Z","close_reason":"Implemented rano binary detection with CAP_NET_ADMIN and /proc access checking","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33wzo","depends_on_id":"bd-308f5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:14:24.487923769Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-349x","title":"Task: Add webhook configuration in ntm.yaml","description":"## Overview
Implement configuration parsing for webhooks in ntm.yaml.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/config/webhooks.go:
   - WebhookConfig struct
   - ParseWebhookConfig(yaml []byte) ([]WebhookConfig, error)
   - ValidateConfig() error

2. Config structure:
   webhooks:
     - name: slack-notifications
       url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
       events:
         - agent.completed
         - agent.error
       formatter: slack
       filter:
         session: myproject*
         agent_type: [claude, codex]
         severity: [warning, error]
       retry:
         max_attempts: 5
         backoff: exponential
       timeout: 30s
       secret: ${NTM_WEBHOOK_SECRET}  # env var substitution

3. Validation rules:
   - URL must be valid HTTP(S)
   - Events must be recognized event types
   - Formatter must be registered
   - Warn on unrecognized options

4. Hot reload:
   - Detect config file changes
   - Reload webhooks without restart
   - Log config changes

## Implementation Notes
- Use mapstructure for YAML parsing
- Support environment variable substitution
- Glob patterns for session filtering

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config parses correctly from ntm.yaml
- Validation catches common errors
- Hot reload works
- Unit tests for config parsing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:50.948504334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:20.358287058Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-349x","depends_on_id":"bd-2o8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:20.358255358Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-349x","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:50.978590620Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,99 @@
+package swarm
+
+import "time"
+
+// ProjectBeadCount represents a project and its open bead count.
+type ProjectBeadCount struct {
+	Path      string `json:"path"`       // Absolute path to project
+	Name      string `json:"name"`       // Project directory name
+	OpenBeads int    `json:"open_beads"` // Count of open beads
+	Tier      int    `json:"tier"`       // Calculated tier (1, 2, or 3)
+}
+
+// ProjectAllocation represents the agent allocation for a single project.
+type ProjectAllocation struct {
+	Project     ProjectBeadCount `json:"project"`
+	CCAgents    int              `json:"cc_agents"`
+	CodAgents   int              `json:"cod_agents"`
+	GmiAgents   int              `json:"gmi_agents"`
+	TotalAgents int              `json:"total_agents"`
+}
+
+// SwarmPlan is the complete execution plan for the weighted swarm.
+type SwarmPlan struct {
+	// Metadata
+	CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
+	ScanDir   string    `json:"scan_dir"`
+
+	// Per-project allocations
+	Allocations []ProjectAllocation `json:"allocations"`
+
+	// Aggregate totals
+	TotalCC     int `json:"total_cc"`
+	TotalCod    int `json:"total_cod"`
+	TotalGmi    int `json:"total_gmi"`
+	TotalAgents int `json:"total_agents"`
+
+	// Session structure
+	SessionsPerType int `json:"sessions_per_type"`
+	PanesPerSession int `json:"panes_per_session"` // Calculated: ceil(total/sessions)
+
+	// Session names to create
+	Sessions []SessionSpec `json:"sessions"`
+}
+
+// SessionSpec describes a tmux session to create.
+type SessionSpec struct {
+	Name      string     `json:"name"`       // e.g., "cc_agents_1"
+	AgentType string     `json:"agent_type"` // "cc", "cod", or "gmi"
+	PaneCount int        `json:"pane_count"`
+	Panes     []PaneSpec `json:"panes"`
+}
+
+// PaneSpec describes a pane within a session.
+type PaneSpec struct {
+	Index      int    `json:"index"`   // 1-based pane index
+	Project    string `json:"project"` // Which project this pane works on
+	AgentType  string `json:"agent_type"`
+	AgentIndex int    `json:"agent_index"` // Agent number within project
+	LaunchCmd  string `json:"launch_cmd"`  // "cc", "cod", or "gmi"
+}
+
+// SwarmState tracks the runtime state of a running swarm.
+type SwarmState struct {
+	Plan       *SwarmPlan           `json:"plan"`
+	StartedAt  time.Time            `json:"started_at"`
+	PaneStates map[string]PaneState `json:"pane_states"` // key: "session:pane"
+	LimitHits  []LimitHitEvent      `json:"limit_hits"`
+	Respawns   []RespawnEvent       `json:"respawns"`
+}
+
+// PaneState tracks individual pane runtime state.
+type PaneState struct {
+	SessionPane  string     `json:"session_pane"` // "cc_agents_1:1.5"
+	AgentType    string     `json:"agent_type"`
+	Project      string     `json:"project"`
+	Status       string     `json:"status"` // "running", "limit_hit", "respawning"
+	LastActivity time.Time  `json:"last_activity"`
+	LimitHitAt   *time.Time `json:"limit_hit_at,omitempty"`
+	RespawnCount int        `json:"respawn_count"`
+}
+
+// LimitHitEvent records when an agent hits usage limits.
+type LimitHitEvent struct {
+	SessionPane string    `json:"session_pane"`
+	AgentType   string    `json:"agent_type"`
+	Project     string    `json:"project"`
+	DetectedAt  time.Time `json:"detected_at"`
+	Pattern     string    `json:"pattern"` // Which pattern matched
+}
+
+// RespawnEvent records agent respawns.
+type RespawnEvent struct {
+	SessionPane     string    `json:"session_pane"`
+	AgentType       string    `json:"agent_type"`
+	RespawnedAt     time.Time `json:"respawned_at"`
+	AccountRotated  bool      `json:"account_rotated"`
+	PreviousAccount string    `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
+	NewAccount      string    `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
+}
